---
title: "Summarize simulated data with duckdb"
subtitle: > 
  duckdb with arrow lets you summarize massive data sets with ease.
author: Kyle Baron
date: 2026-05-30
image: images/duckdb.png
categories: 
- duckdb
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Introduction
This is a quick blog post showing how you can summarize a very 
large set of simulations using duckdb. You can learn more about 
duckdb [here](https://duckdb.org/). The duckdb 
[R package](https://cran.r-project.org/package=duckdb) can take a long time to 
install, but will be worth it once your simulations grow out of control. You 
can get mrgsim.ds [here](https://github.com/p-emex/mrgsim.ds).

# Setup 

```{r}
#| message: false
library(mrgsim.ds)
library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(mirai)
library(dplyr)
library(arrow)
```


```{r}
#| echo: false
set.seed(1234)
post <- data.frame(
  TVCL = rnorm(2000, 1, 0.20), 
  TVV = rnorm(2000, 24, 5), 
  TVKA = rnorm(2000, 0.5, 0.1)
)
postfile <- file.path(tempdir(), "post.csv")
data.table::fwrite(x = post, file = postfile)
```


Set up a large simulation; we'll check the number of rows in the 
output once it finishes.

- 3000 subjects
- 2 dose levels
- 28 days of dosing
- Sample every hour

```{r}
mod <- modlib_ds("popex")

mod <- update(
  mod, 
  outvars = "IPRED", 
  end = 4*168, 
  delta = 1, 
  add = seq(144, 168, 0.1)
)

data <- expand.ev(
  ID = 1:3000,
  amt = c(300, 1000), 
  ii = 24, 
  addl = 27
)

data <- mutate(data, dose = amt)

post <- read.csv(postfile)

head(data)

head(post)
```

# Simulate

Simulate 1000 replicates.

```{r}
#| eval: false
unlink("temp-post", recursive = TRUE)

daemons(7, seed = 3579)

everywhere(library(mrgsim.ds))

out <- mirai_map(

  1:1000,
  
  \(x, mod, data, post) {
    
    mod <- param(mod, post[x,])
    
    mrgsim_ds(mod, data, tags = list(rep = x), recover = "dose")
  
  },

  .args = list(mod = mod, data = data, post = post)

)[.]

daemons(0)

out <- reduce_ds(out)

save_ds(out, "temp-post/duckdb.rds")
```

There are 5 billion rows in this simulation set across 1000 parquet files 
totaling 41 Gb of data on disk. 

```{r}
sims <- read_ds("temp-post/duckdb.rds")

sims
```

Note that all your simulated data is now safely stored on disk. If your R 
session crashes or restarts for whatever reason, you can easily restart and 
pick up right where you left off without having to re-run the expensive 
simulation step.

It is also important to note that the data are stored in highly-compressed 
parquet files; it's only the simulation output object (providing access to that
data) that is stored in `.rds` format. So the `.rds` file is very light. 

```{r}
fs::file_size("temp-post/duckdb.rds")
```


```{r}
#| echo: false
arrow::set_io_thread_count(8)
```


## Summarize using arrow / duckdb pipeline

Summarize using duckdb; we'll take median, 5th and 95th percentiles of the 
Week 4 trough concentration by dose and replicate. We can do this in about 
15 seconds on my macbook pro m1 with omp.

To do this, we work in the Apache Arrow R package and convert the arrow data 
set to a duckdb object first, without copying any data.

```{r}
library(arrow)

system.time({ 

summ <- 
  sims %>% 
  as_duckdb_ds() %>% 
  filter(time == 672) %>% 
  group_by(time, rep, dose) %>% 
  summarise(
    n = n(), 
    Lower = quantile(IPRED, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Median = median(IPRED, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Upper = quantile(IPRED, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE), 
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% arrange(
    time, 
    rep, 
    dose
  ) %>% collect()

})

summ
```


Another summary: generate a 95% confidence interval around Cmax after the 
seventh dose.

```{r}

system.time({ 

summ2 <- 
  sims %>% 
  as_duckdb_ds() %>% 
  filter(time >= 144 & time <= 168) %>%
  summarise(
    Cmax = max(IPRED, na.rm = TRUE), 
    .by = c(ID, rep, dose)
  ) %>% 
  compute() %>% 
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    Median = median(Cmax, na.rm = TRUE), 
    .by = c(rep, dose)
  ) %>% 
  arrange(
    rep, 
    dose
  ) %>% collect()
  
})

summ2
```

There appears to be substantial uncertainty in the median Cmax.

```{r}
summ2 %>% 
  summarise(
    n = n(), 
    Lower = quantile(Median, 0.025), 
    Med = median(Median),
    Upper = quantile(Median, 0.975), 
    .by = dose
  )
```


# Summarize using duckdb

This summary uses straight duckdb, writing the summary specification in SQL
code. I've found some applications where this is slightly faster than the 
arrow / duckdb pipeline; in this example, the timing is identical.

```{r}
#| eval: false
con <- dbConnect(duckdb())

ds <- as_arrow_ds(sims)

duckdb_register_arrow(con, "sims", ds)

system.time(
  
  summ3 <- dbGetQuery(con, "
    SELECT
      dose,
      time,
      rep, 
      COUNT(*)                     AS n,
      QUANTILE_CONT(IPRED, 0.025)  AS Lower,
      QUANTILE_CONT(IPRED, 0.5)    AS Median,
      QUANTILE_CONT(IPRED, 0.975)  AS Upper
    FROM sims
    WHERE time = 672
    GROUP BY time, rep, dose
    ORDER BY time, rep, dose
  ")

)

dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

as_tibble(summ3) %>% arrange(time, rep, dose)
```
